metadata.Scope note: Debates introduced by a backbench MP, often on local or personal issues.

field.name: Motion made and Question proposed. That this House do now adjourn

metadata.Scope note: Adopted EU legislation (decisions, directives and regulations).

European material produced by EU institutions

European material produced by EU institutions

metadata.Scope note: Comprehensive report on an organisation's activities throughout the preceding year. Typically annual reports include reports from the Chair, CEO and auditors; mission statement; compliance statements and information on corporate responsibilities; and financial statements and accounts.
Commons debates on business chosen by the Backbench Business Committee.
20 bills presented by backbench winners of the annual ballot.

Briefings on bills and other legislation. Note that filtering by this content type currently retrieves all relevant items from March 2013 only; to retrieve earlier briefings on bills, please use the Commons Briefing papers content type, and/or add "Bill" as a title keyword to your search query.
Annual four or five day debate to discuss the resolutions proposed in the Budget speech.
Questions following the weekly business statement.
See also Business questions.

metadata.Decision/discussion history note: LT swapped from General Synod measure and UF General Synod measures added. 18 Sep 2014
metadata.Scope note: Instruments by which changes are made to legislation relating to the administration and organisation of the Church of England.
Papers presented to Parliament by the government.
Used for the committee stage of bills in the House of Commons.

Used for the committee stage of bills in the House of Lords Chamber.  

For committee stages of bills taken in Lords Grand Committee, use the Debates on Bills content type combined with Chamber/Committee House of Lords Grand Committee.
Verbatim reports of committee sessions.
Papers providing in-depth and impartial analysis on every major piece of primary legislation and on other topics of public and parliamentary concern. Regular statistics papers are also published.
Collections of parliamentary and other relevant material produced for some of the main debates in the House of Commons.
Bill that seeks to set out the law in a particular subject area in a clearer and more up-to-date form without changing its substance.
Briefings produced by the House of Commons Library to help MPs and caseworkers with questions from constituents.
Papers produced for public comment on the making or amending of legislation.
Observations on consultations, presented by government departments to either House.

Statements made under the European Union (Withdrawal) Act 2018. Where an instrument or draft creates a criminal offence, a ministerial statement required by the Act must (among other things) include an explanation of why, in the relevant Minister’s opinion, there are good reasons for creating the offence and for the penalty provided in respect of it.

Formal discussion on a topic in the House of Commons or House of Lords. Note that, currently, filtering by this content type may exclude content from sessions earlier than 12/13. To retrieve earlier debates, please use the Parliamentary proceedings content type.

metadata.Decision/discussion history note: Added for testing of CPIMF attributes. 28 Feb 2012
metadata.Scope note:
Note that filtering by this content type currently retrieves debates in either chamber from the beginning of the 05/06 session only. Proceedings in General or Standing committee are retrieved from the 92/93 session.

To retrieve earlier debates, please use the Parliamentary proceedings content type.
Any debate on a treaty.
Legislation made under powers conferred by an act of Parliament, to make detailed arrangements which expand on broader provisions in the parent act.
From the beginning of the 2006-07 session, Standing Committees were renamed General Committees. This filter includes both Standing and General Committee proceedings.
Papers that are placed in the library of either House by a minister or the Speaker.
A form of secondary legislation under powers granted by the Deregulation and Contracting Out Act 1994. Superseded by Regulatory reform orders in 2001.

Proposals for a draft deregulation order under the Deregulation and Contracting Out Act 1994, laid by ministers to be considered by Parliament for 60 days, including scrutiny by select committees. After 60 days the minister could lay the draft order itself for further scrutiny, reporting details of any representations and changes made to the proposal.

Proposals for a draft regulatory reform order under the Regulatory Reform Act 2001, laid by ministers to be considered by Parliament for 60 days, including scrutiny by select committees. After 60 days the minister can lay the draft order itself for further scrutiny, reporting details of any representations and changes made to the proposal.

metadata.Decision/discussion history note: LT changed from Proposals for a draft remedial order to match other proposals. 9 Sep 2024
metadata.Scope note: Proposals for a draft remedial order under the Human Rights Act 1998, laid by ministers to be considered by Parliament for 60 days, including scrutiny by the Joint Committee on Human Rights. After 60 days the minister can lay the draft order itself for further scrutiny, reporting details of any representations and changes made to the proposal.

field.name: Proposals for a draft remedial order

metadata.Scope note: Note that filtering by this content type currently retrieves all relevant items from March 2015 only; to retrieve earlier Economic indicators, please use the Commons Briefing papers content type, and/or add "economic indicators" as title keywords to your search query.

metadata.Decision/discussion history note: LT swapped from Standing Order No 24 applications, and changed from SIT to CTP. 10 June 2024
metadata.Scope note: Applications made under Standing Order No 24 for an emergency debate in the House of Commons. (Standing Order No 24 was renumbered from No 20 in May 1997.)
Debates in the House of Commons on a specific and important matter that should have urgent consideration. Applied for under Standing Order No 24.
Three days in the course of a Commons session when the estimates are approved.

European Union documents deposited in Parliament for scrutiny. Deposited documents include:
(a) a document submitted by an institution of the European Union to another institution and put by either into the public domain;
(b) a draft legislative act or a proposal for amendment of such an act; and
(c) a draft decision relating to the Common Foreign and Security Policy of the European Union under Title V of the Treaty on European
Union.

term.name: European material produced by EU institutions

metadata.Scope note: European Union material including draft legislation, consultative documents, European Parliament reports and recommendations, adopted legislation, and miscellaneous EU documents.

metadata.Decision/discussion history note: SN changed to explain that non-leg EP reports will no longer be created. 7 June 2017
metadata.Scope note:
European Parliament Committee reports (including resolutions) or recommendations. 

Records for Non-legislative European Parliament reports will no longer be created as of 19/06/2017.

European material produced by EU institutions

metadata.Scope note: EU documents and information relating to the UK scrutiny of EU documents.

metadata.Scope note: Documents received from UK government departments describing the content of and Government policy towards European deposited documents.

metadata.Scope note: Correspondence with Ministers in relation to the scrutiny of deposited documents or published reports.

Proposals, drafts and initiatives for legislation.

term.name: Examiners of Petitions for Private Bills reports

metadata.Scope note: Examiners of Petitions for Private Bills are appointed in both Houses to examine each petition against a Bill and to certify that the standing orders relating to private business have been complied with in each case. The Examiners may report to the Houses where the standing orders relating to private business have not been complied with.

term.displayName: Examiners of Petitions for Private Bills reports
term.creator: morrelln

field.name: Examiners of Petitions for Private Bills
Examiners of Petitions for Private Bills report
Examiners of Petitions for Private Bills reports

metadata.Decision/discussion history note:
SN changed. 18 June 2018

LT changed from Prayers (Fatal). 3 July 2023
metadata.Scope note: Members can table motions in the form of a "prayer" to annul or withdraw negative statutory instruments. A motion to annul, if tabled and and passed within 40 days of the instrument being laid, is fatal to the progress of the instrument. In the House of Commons, prayer motions are usually tabled as early day motions.
A proceeding on a motion decided without debate.
Bills reflecting government commitments.

Proceedings of the Scottish, Welsh and Northern Ireland Grand Committees, and of regional Grand Committees in 2009. See Legislative Grand Committee proceedings (HC) for the English votes for English laws process from 2016 to 2020.

Publications ordered to be printed by the House of Commons including parliamentary standards, accounts, management, standing orders and MPs' financial interests. These papers are not laid into the House or reported by select committee. They are produced by the House of Commons about the House of Commons.

field.name: Independent Assessor reports on environmental statement responses
Examiners of Petitions for Private Bills reports

metadata.Scope note: The Sessional Returns document provides the definitive summary of the work of the House of Commons and its committees over the course of a parliamentary session, and is published by the House itself shortly after the end of each parliamentary year. It includes figures for the number and length of all sittings and the time spent on different types of business in the Chamber and Westminster Hall; as well as comprehensive data on bills, parliamentary questions, divisions, early day motions and the meetings and reports of Commons committees.

Publications ordered to be printed by the House of Lords including annual reports, business plans, the financial and other interests of Lords and their staff, the peers' reimbursement scheme and standing orders. These papers are not laid into the House or reported by select committee. They are produced by the House of Lords about the House of Lords.
The House of Lords Journal is the corrected, archive edition of Minutes of Proceedings, the formal record of House of Lords business for a given day.
Hybrid bills mix the characteristics of public and private bills.

Occasionally an SI which needs to be approved by both Houses (an affirmative instrument) affects some members of a group (be it individuals or bodies), more than others in the same group.  When this happens the SI is ruled to be a hybrid instrument.

metadata.Decision/discussion history note: IN added after discussion at Indexing and PQ meeting. 15 Feb 2018
metadata.Scope note: Formerly known as Compliance cost assessments and Regulatory impact assessments. New IA format was introduced on 14 May 2007 and the RIA was replaced from November 2007.

term.name: Independent Assessor reports on environmental statement responses

metadata.Scope note: Reports providing an independent summary of feedback received in response to public consultation on environmental statements for proposed hybrid legislation. Independent assessors are appointed by the Examiners of Petitions for Private Bills under House of Commons Private Business Standing Order 224A and House of Lords Standing Order 83A.

term.displayName: Independent Assessor reports on environmental statement responses
term.creator: gormanp

field.name: Independent Assessor reports on environmental statement responses

term.name: Instruments coming into operation before being laid

metadata.Scope note: Letters notifying that Statutory instruments are coming into operation without being laid before Parliament, usually by reason of a Parliamentary recess.

term.creator: admin

metadata.Scope note: Debates to discuss the government programme proposed in the King's speech.

metadata.Decision/discussion history note: Added for testing of CPIMF attributes. 28 Feb 2012
metadata.Scope note:
Debates held on the government's legislative programme, both primary legislation (bills) and delegated legislation. 

See also content type Legislative formal proceedings.
A legislative proceeding on a motion decided without debate. See also Legislative debates.
Committee proceedings under the English votes for English laws process from 2016 to 2020.
A form of secondary legislation under powers granted by the Legislative and Regulatory Reform Act 2006.
A category of private acts; local acts relate to matters of local interest.

Type of statutory instrument. Under the Localism Act 2011 (sections 5, 6 and 7), ministers can use localism orders to change or repeal a part of the law that prevents a local authority from exercising the general power of competence (a power that allows local authorities to do anything that an individual may do) or overlaps with that power.
Statements made by the Lord Speaker on matters affecting the House of Lords.
Collections of reading material, intended to help Members prepare for debates or other business of the House of Lords. Lords Briefing packs ceased to be used in July 2019.

metadata.Scope note: Contributions to parliamentary proceedings and oral question time.

metadata.Decision/discussion history note:
Procedural term attribute added. We are unable to add subtypes to Written statements in indexing but would like to be able to tag those which are Ministerial corrections as such. The attribute is a workaround. Sep 2022

SN changed to include usage in the Lords. 10 Sep 2024
metadata.Scope note: Corrections to the official record by ministers and other members who provide information to Parliament in an official capacity. Published on the Written Questions, Answers and Statements website from 2014-15 (both Houses), and previously printed in the Official Report from 2007-08 (House of Commons only).
Oral statements delivered by ministers.
Debates to discuss the government programme proposed in the monarch's speech.

National Policy Statements are planning documents that provide the primary basis for making decisions on development consent order applications for nationally significant infrastructure projects. A final proposal is laid before Parliament under Section 9(8) of the Planning Act 2008. This is subject to parliamentary procedure.

National Policy Statements are planning documents that provide the primary basis for making decisions on development consent order applications for nationally significant infrastructure projects. An initial proposal is laid before Parliament under Section 9(2) of the Planning Act 2008. This is not subject to procedure but is subject to a consultation period.

European material produced by EU institutions

metadata.Decision/discussion history note:
SN changed. 18 June 2018

LT changed from Prayers (Non-fatal). 3 July 2023
metadata.Scope note: Members can table motions in the form of a "prayer" to annul or withdraw negative statutory instruments. A motion to withdraw is not fatal to the progress of the instrument. In the House of Commons, prayer motions are usually tabled as early day motions.
Miscellaneous EU reports and communications; selected non-scrutiny material found in the Official Journal “C” series.

field.name: European material produced by EU institutions
European material produced by EU institutions

metadata.Scope note: A non-legislative proceeding on a motion decided without debate.

term.name: Notification of a published draft instrument under European Union (Withdrawal) Act 2018

metadata.Scope note: As set out in Paragraph 14 of Schedule 8 of the European Union (Withdrawal) Act 2018, a two-stage affirmative procedure applies to any instruments that amend or revoke secondary legislation originally made under the European Communities Act 1972. The relevant authority must publish a draft of the instrument at least 28 days before the instrument or draft is laid. Parliament must be notified when the draft is published.

term.displayName: Notification of a published draft instrument under European Union (Withdrawal) Act 2018
term.creator: sunleyj

field.name: Notification of a published draft instrument under EUWA
Notification to Parliament of a published draft instrument pursuant to Paragraph 14 of Schedule 8 of the European Union (Withdrawal) Act 2018
Notification of a published draft instrument under European Union (Withdrawal) Act 2018

metadata.Scope note: Observations by government departments in response to Public (paper) petitions presented in either Chamber.
Twenty days per session allocated to debates on subjects of interest to the Opposition.
An intervention, or a resumed question (if the question continues following an intervention). Also used for all Speaker contributions except disallowed questions.
To include the text of answers to oral questions in your search, please also select content type Oral answers to questions, under Members' contributions.
Statements made on the floor of either Chamber.
Written corrections made by Members of the House of Commons not holding a government position.

A petition is a formal written request sent in to Parliament by members of the public calling for some form of action by the Government. Paper petitions must be presented on the petitioners' behalf by an MP or member of the Lords.

metadata.Decision/discussion history note:
LT changed from Parliamentary information lists to reflect change of content type on the website in November 2018. 8 Jan 2019

LT changed from Parliamentary facts and figures to reflect change of series name on the website. 13 Mar 2023
metadata.Scope note: The 'Parliament: facts and figures' series covers topics including elections, government, legislation, Members and parliamentary business.
Publications produced by or required by Parliament for its work.

field.name: Transport and Works Act order applications

metadata.Scope note: Debates and other proceedings which take place in the Chambers of the House of Commons and House of Lords and in committee.
To include the text of answers to oral questions in your search, please also select content type Oral answers to questions, under Members' contributions.
A category of private acts; personal acts relate to particular persons, and are nowadays very rare.
Formal written requests sent to Parliament by members of the public calling for government action, and observations by government departments thereon.
Interruption of proceedings by a Member to appeal to the Chair for guidance or a ruling on a matter of order or procedure.

metadata.Decision/discussion history note: LT changed from Points of order following indexer review discussion. 4 June 2025
metadata.Scope note: Appeals to the Chair for guidance or a ruling on a matter of order or procedure, typically heard after questions, urgent questions, statements and Standing Order No 24 applications.

Responsive policy briefings from the Parliament Office of Science and Technology based on mini-literature reviews and peer review, typically commissioned by select committees and library research services.

Proactive four-page policy briefings from the Parliamentary Office of Science and Technology based on reviews of the research literature, interviews with stakeholders and peer review, commissioned by the POST Board.

metadata.Decision/discussion history note:
SN changed. 18 June 2018

UF Prayer motion added. 16 July 2018
metadata.Scope note: Early day motion praying that a statutory instrument or other piece of delegated legislation be not made - if the instrument is in draft; be annulled - if subject to the negative procedure in the House; or be revoked -if the instrument has already been passed by the House.
A bill presented at the Table, notice having been given on the Order Paper.
Oral questions asked of the Prime Minister by the leader of the Opposition and back-benchers.
An act which confers private or particular rights or is local and personal in its effect.
A bill which confers private or particular rights or is local and personal in its effect.
Public bill brought forward by a backbench MP rather than by the government.
Private members' bills introduced in the Lords.
Questions of urgent importance asked of the government in the Lords Chamber. Renamed as Urgent questions in the Commons from 2002/03.

When making regulations under powers in the EU (Withdrawal) Act 2018, a Minister may choose  either the negative or affirmative procedure in most cases.  When the Minister proposes to use the negative procedure, the statutory instrument (SI)  must be laid in draft for consideration, also known as “sifting”, by the European Statutory Instrument Committee in the Commons and the Secondary Legislation Scrutiny Committee in the Lords. At this stage the SI is called a proposed negative statutory instrument.
An act having a general effect in some or all of the constituent parts of the UK.  Does not include Acts of the devolved Administrations.
Includes proceedings of public bill committees in the House of Commons and special public bill committees in the House of Lords.
A bill having a general effect in some or all of the constituent parts of the UK.

Type of statutory instrument. Under the Public Bodies Act 2011, ministers can use public bodies orders to abolish, merge or change the constitutional or funding arrangements of certain public bodies. The Act sets out tests that must be met: for example, whether the change improves accountability.
Debates to discuss the government programme proposed in the Queen's speech.
Short debates in the House of Lords. These were called 'unstarred questions' prior to the 2006-07 session.

metadata.Decision/discussion history note: SN changed following advice from Journal Office. 21 Oct 2015
metadata.Scope note: A form of secondary legislation under powers granted by the Regulatory Reform Act 2001. Largely replaced by Legislative reform orders in 2006.

An order made by a Minister under the Human Rights Act 1998 to amend legislation which has been found to be incompatible with the European Convention on Human Rights. Remedial orders can be used to amend both primary and secondary legislation, and they may do anything necessary to fix the incompatibility with the Convention rights.
Verbatim proceedings of inquiry sessions conducted by Commons, Lords and Joint Select Committees. Lords select committee oral evidence added from 2021-22 session.
Formal reply to a Select Committee report provided by a government department or a public body.

field.name: Government responses to Select Committee reports

metadata.Scope note: Introduced in December 2013, a statement made on the publication of a select committee report or announcement of an inquiry.
A decision by the Speaker of the Commons or any other occupant of the Chair in the Chamber or Westminster Hall, on a matter of order or procedure.
Statements made from the Chair on matters affecting the operation of Parliament.
Formal statements made orally or in writing in either Chamber.

metadata.Decision/discussion history note: UF Orders and regulations added after discussion at PQ meeting. 4 Nov 2015
metadata.Scope note: One type of delegated legislation, to supplement what is set down in the text of an act for reasons of detail and flexibility.
Bills introduced by leave of the House following a speech of not more than ten minutes from the sponsor of the proposal.
Debates on topical issues raised by Members taking place in the Commons.

Topical questions in the House of Commons are questions that may be asked during the last 15 minutes of most ministerial question sessions. Introduced in the 2007-08 session, they allow MPs to ask a minister about anything they have responsibility for without having to provide advance notice of the question.

term.name: Transport and Works Act order applications

metadata.Decision/discussion history note: LT changed from Transport and Works Act orders. 4 Jan 2022
metadata.Scope note: Applications made for a Transport and Works Act Order, under the Transport and Works Act 1992. Orders under the Transport and Works Act 1992 can authorise guided transport schemes, such as railways and tramways, and certain other types of infrastructure project in England and Wales. Under SI 2006/1466 reg.13(1), copies of the application and related documents are deposited in the House of Commons Library and, unless the Secretary of State directs otherwise, in the House of Lords Library.

term.displayName: Transport and Works Act order applications
term.creator: somervillea

field.name: Transport and Works Act order application
Transport and Works Act order applications

metadata.Scope note: International agreements laid by command.
Material relating to UK scrutiny of EU proposals and consultative documents.
These are papers which are laid “by Command” as opposed to “by Act”.  They include statements about gifts or guarantees made by government departments and some annual reports.

field.name: Notification of a published draft instrument under European Union (Withdrawal) Act 2018

metadata.Decision/discussion history note: UF Unnumbered Act papers added in response to data in new Papers Laid feed. 17 Nov 2017
metadata.Scope note: Unprinted papers are laid before each House but are not ordered to be printed as a House of Commons or Lords Paper.

field.name: Instruments coming into operation before being laid

metadata.Scope note: Questions of urgent importance asked of the government in the Commons Chamber. Renamed from Private notice questions from 2002/03. See Private notice questions in the Lords.
Published statement of government policy.

metadata.Decision/discussion history note: LT changed from Ministerial corrections following a change in procedure for corrections to allow written corrections from Members who are not ministers. 8 Mar 2024
metadata.Scope note: Corrections to the official record by Members of Parliament.
Questions receiving a written answer.
Statements delivered in written form by ministers.
